Insights.Title: Insights
Insights.Subtitle: A lightweight explanation layer for the current result set.

QuickSummary.Description: The basic shape of this result.
QuickSummary.Title: {kind} · {rows} rows · {columns} columns · {chartStatus}

KeyInsights.Description: What is most worth checking first in this result.
KeyInsights.Empty: No clear pattern stands out yet. Try filtering or charting this result.

Card.OutlierMaxLine: The highest rows can distort the overall read.
Card.LongTailLine: The distribution has a long tail that can mislead aggregate analysis.
Card.RiskLine: {value} takes about {share} of the current result.
Card.LowInformationHeadline: {column} has no useful variance in this result
Card.LowInformationLine: {count} rows are concentrated in {value} ({share}), so this field has no information gain for distribution analysis.
Card.DimensionLine: {value} stands out as the dominant segment.

Decision.OutlierTitle: A small set of high {column} rows is pulling the result upward
Decision.OutlierImpact: Average-based analysis can become misleading unless you inspect those rows first.
Decision.RiskTitle: {column} points to a concentrated risk segment
Decision.RiskImpact: The next step should isolate the segment before summarizing the whole result.
Decision.LowInformationTitle: {column} does not add enough analytical signal
Decision.LowInformationImpact: Continuing on this field will not explain the result, so move to a richer dimension.
Decision.ConcentrationImpact: This concentration can hide differences between segments, so break it down before drawing conclusions.
Decision.TrendTitle: {column} should drive the next time-based check
Decision.TrendImpact: A trend view can reveal whether the result is stable or driven by a specific period.
Decision.DefaultImpact: Use the recommended first action to turn this result into a more precise next result.

Messages.LowInformationDimension: {column} lacks variance: {count} rows are concentrated in {value} ({share}). Switch to another dimension for the next step.
Messages.RiskCategory: {value} accounts for about {share} in {column}, which is worth checking first.
Messages.TopDimension: {value} is one of the most active values in {column} ({count} rows).
Messages.TimeTrend: This result is well-suited for a time trend on {column} to spot spikes and shifts.
Messages.MeasureSpread: {column} has a long-tail shape that can distort aggregate analysis.
Messages.HighCardinality: {column} looks high-cardinality, so it is better used for filtering than headline summaries.
Messages.SpikePattern: {bucket} stands out as a period that can drive the overall trend.
Messages.DropPattern: {bucket} falls below the surrounding pattern and can change the trend reading.
Messages.OutlierPattern: {column} contains extreme values that can distort the summary.
Messages.CorrelationPattern: {left} and {right} tend to move together in this sample (correlation {correlation}).

KeyColumns.Description: Columns you are most likely to use next.

RecommendedActions.Description: The next step is ordered for you.
RecommendedActions.Empty: No ready-to-run action is available yet.

MainFinding.Label: Main finding
MainFinding.Recommendation: Recommended next step: focus on the strongest signal first.

ActionPrompts.TimeErrorTrend: Use the current result and SQL ({sql}) to suggest the next query for tracking error trends over time with {timeColumn}. Explain what to group by and why.
ActionPrompts.ServiceErrorBreakdown: Use the current result and SQL ({sql}) to suggest the next query for breaking down errors by {serviceColumn}. Keep the recommendation focused and practical.
ActionPrompts.TopMessages: Use the current result and SQL ({sql}) to suggest the next query for reviewing top values in {messageColumn}. Mention the best chart or table view to use.
ActionPrompts.PatternFollowUp: Use the current result and SQL ({sql}) to investigate this pattern: {pattern}. Focus on columns {columns}. Suggest the next query and explain why it should help.
ActionPrompts.ExplainResult: Explain what this result means in plain language, what stands out, and what to ask next. Base your answer on the current result and SQL ({sql}).

Analysis.GeneratedSqlDescription: SQL used to reproduce this analysis result.
Analysis.VisualResultEmpty: No chartable result rows are available for this analysis.
Analysis.NoGeneratedSql: No generated SQL is available for this analysis.

AiDecision.DefaultDescription: AI selected the next analysis step from the Profile.
AiDecision.InvalidTitle: This result is not analyzable yet
AiDecision.InvalidDescription: The result is empty or lacks usable fields. Adjust the SQL or expand the range first.
AiDecision.InvalidReason: The current result does not have enough evidence for continued analysis.
AiDecision.LowDensityDescription: {column} has low information density. Move to a more useful aggregation.
AiDecision.NextAxisDescription: Based on the Profile, {column} is a better next analysis axis.
AiDecision.GroupByGoal: Identify which {column} contributes the most activity.
AiDecision.LowDensityReason: {column} is too concentrated to explain the result well.
AiDecision.NextAxisReason: Choose {column} as the highest-value next analysis dimension.

SuggestionDescriptions.ViewTimeTrend: Break the result down over time to check whether it clusters in specific periods.
SuggestionDescriptions.GroupByService: Break down by service or source to find the largest contributor.
SuggestionDescriptions.AnalyzeSource: Break down by the most relevant dimension to locate the main source.
SuggestionDescriptions.TopMessages: Find the most frequent content to see what drives this result.
SuggestionDescriptions.InspectOutliers: Review the highest-value samples to understand where outliers come from.
SuggestionDescriptions.ViewDistribution: Quantify the main measure distribution, tail, and concentration.
SuggestionDescriptions.FilterOutliers: Narrow the result to the anomalous subset for continued analysis.
SuggestionDescriptions.PatternFollowUp: Follow up on the strongest pattern and compare related segments.
SuggestionDescriptions.Default: Continue drilling into the current result.
SuggestionDescriptions.ViewTimeTrendWithColumn: Check whether the issue changes over {column}.
SuggestionDescriptions.TopMessagesWithColumn: Find the most frequent {column} values to see what drives this result.
SuggestionDescriptions.ViewDistributionWithColumn: Find the highest values and quantify the long tail for {column}.
SuggestionDescriptions.FilterOutliersWithColumn: Keep abnormal rows and continue analyzing them by {column}.

SuggestionGoals.TopMessages: See which text values appear most often.
SuggestionGoals.GroupByService: Locate the source of the anomaly.
SuggestionGoals.AnalyzeSource: Find the segment behind the issue.

Server.RecommendedAnalysisGoal: Move to the next useful analysis step after confirmation.
Server.RecommendedAnalysisDescription: Run the confirmed recommended SQL and summarize whether the result is more useful.
Server.ReadOnlySelectError: Recommended SQL must be a single read-only SELECT statement.
Server.NoRecommendedRowsLimitation: The recommended SQL returned no rows. Adjust the direction or expand the range.
Server.LeaderSummaryWithValue: {leader} is the most prominent group in this result, with {value} records.
Server.LeaderSummary: {leader} is the group most worth checking first.
Server.NoRecommendedRowsSummary: The recommended SQL did not return an analyzable result.
Server.NoRecommendedRowsHeadline: The next analysis returned no result

Rag.Description: Retrieve the most relevant document snippets for the user's question and return context passages for answering.

ChartBuilder.Description:
Return a chart configuration based on the provided data.

Usage:
1. Specify chartType (bar/line/area/pie).
2. data is an array of query result rows.
3. If you are unsure about xKey / yKeys / categoryKey / valueKey, leave them empty and the tool will infer:
   - Line/area: prefer a time field for x, numeric fields for y.
   - Bar: prefer a category field for x, numeric fields for y.
   - Pie: choose one category field as categoryKey and one numeric field as valueKey.
4. The tool infers time/numeric/category columns and sets options.xKeyType/sortBy when appropriate.

SqlRunner.Description:
A tool to execute read-only SQL queries on the current database connection.
Usage:
- Only accepts a single SELECT / SHOW / DESCRIBE / DESC / EXPLAIN statement; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.
- For questions about queries, aggregations, reports, or metrics, generate SQL first, call this tool, then answer from results.
- Always match the current database dialect.
- If schema is unclear, prefer the provided schema context first, then use dialect-appropriate metadata queries to inspect it.
- Do not use MySQL-only DESCRIBE / SHOW COLUMNS syntax on PostgreSQL.
SqlRunner.MissingConnection: Missing database connection info; unable to execute query.
SqlRunner.ReadOnlyOnly: Only read-only queries are allowed (SELECT/SHOW/DESCRIBE/DESC/EXPLAIN/WITH/PRAGMA). Do not include write or update statements.
SqlRunner.SelectAllNotAllowed: SELECT * is not allowed in chatbot-generated SQL. Select only the columns needed for the answer.
SqlRunner.OrderByIndexMissing: This query may be expensive: ORDER BY uses column(s) {columns} on table {table}, and they are not confirmed to be indexed. Narrow the selected columns or inspect indexes first.
SqlRunner.OrderByIndexUnknown: This query may be expensive: ORDER BY uses column(s) {columns} on table {table}, but index support could not be confirmed. Narrow the selected columns or inspect indexes first.

Issues.PartSizeTooSmall: Average part size is too small; many small files
Issues.PartSizeTooLarge: Very large part detected; may affect merges and performance
Issues.PartFragmented: Part sizes are uneven; fragmentation risk
Issues.TtlMissing: Large table without TTL may accumulate historical data
Issues.TooManyActiveMutations: Many active mutations may impact cluster resources
